spec_match.rationale: Separates the standardized autocorrect IDL/attribute reflection from the live beforeinput/input stream that can expose real text-edit behavior.

interactive.rationale: The page has typeable on/off fields, focus controls, a clear action, live current values, and an event log for input activity.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page frames itself as a cross-browser support matrix with a live probe that distinguishes IDL support from OS/browser autocorrection observation.

edge_cases.rationale: The probe now logs beforeinput/input activity, inputType values when exposed, current field values, composition events, and replacement-style autocorrection observations separately from IDL support.

references.rationale: The concept page includes the canonical chromestatus.com/feature/6264645053710336 link.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: Resolved the prior text-edit and reference gaps by adding a beforeinput/input event log with inputType/current-value reporting, explicit OS autocorrection observation language, and the ChromeStatus link. Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp: both probe fields logged beforeinput/input insertText events, readouts showed current values/inputTypes, and console/network were clean.
